title: Ramnagar Fort
tagline: Ramnagar Fort — heritage city of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Ramnagar Fort is a fort at the town of Ramnagar in the Udhampur district of Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir.
overview.description: Ramnagar Fort is a fort at the town of Ramnagar in the Udhampur district of Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir. It is believed to have been built by Rajput Raja Suchet Singh, who died in 1844. His wife performed sati nearby. There is a Samadhi of Maharani at the site where the sati was performed.

topPlaces.name: Ramnagar, Varanasi
topPlaces.description: Ramnagar is a town, just adjacent to Varanasi city and a municipal board in Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Ramnagar has a fort known as Ramnagar Fort which is still the residence of King of Varanasi (Benares).

topPlaces.name: Kachhua Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Kachhua Sanctuary is in Varanasi district in Uttar Pradesh, India. Turtles, the Ganges dolphin and other water animals can be found here.

topPlaces.name: Bhagawanpur, Varanasi
topPlaces.description: Bhagawanpur is a census town in Varanasi tehsil of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The census town and village falls under the Gourdih gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Durga Mandir, Ramnagar
topPlaces.description: The Durga Mandir (Temple) is situated in Ramnagar of Banaras (or Varanasi). It is believed that it was built 500 years ago which is under control of royal family of Banaras State currently.

topPlaces.name: Assi River
topPlaces.description: The Assi (Hindi: अस्सी) or Asi (Hindi: असी Asī or असि Asi [ˈʌsi]) is a small river in the city of Varanasi in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is a minor tributary of the river Ganges.

topPlaces.name: Sant Ravidas Ghat
topPlaces.description: Sant Ravidas Ghat is the southernmost and largest ghat in Varanasi. To most visitors to Varanasi, it is known for being an important religious place for Ravidasis with a 25 acres park known as Sant Ravidas Smarak Park.

topPlaces.name: Mahila Maha Vidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Mahila Maha Vidyalaya (MMV), also known as Women's College, Banaras Hindu University, is a women's college in the Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, India which offers undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral courses in various subjects to women. It was founded in 1929 by Mahamana Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya.

topPlaces.name: Assi Ghat
topPlaces.description: Assi Ghat is the southernmost ghat in Varanasi, India. To most visitors to Varanasi, it is known for being a place where long-term foreign students, researchers, and tourists live.

items.note: Ramnagar is a town, just adjacent to Varanasi city and a municipal board in Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Ramnagar has a fort known as Ramnagar Fort which is still the residence of King of Varanasi (Benares).
Kachhua Sanctuary is in Varanasi district in Uttar Pradesh, India. Turtles, the Ganges dolphin and other water animals can be found here.
Bhagawanpur is a census town in Varanasi tehsil of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The census town and village falls under the Gourdih gram panchayat.
The Durga Mandir (Temple) is situated in Ramnagar of Banaras (or Varanasi). It is believed that it was built 500 years ago which is under control of royal family of Banaras State currently.
The Assi (Hindi: अस्सी) or Asi (Hindi: असी Asī or असि Asi [ˈʌsi]) is a small river in the city of Varanasi in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is a minor tributary of the river Ganges.

Sant Ravidas Ghat is the southernmost and largest ghat in Varanasi. To most visitors to Varanasi, it is known for being an important religious place for Ravidasis with a 25 acres park known as Sant Ravidas Smarak Park.

Mahila Maha Vidyalaya (MMV), also known as Women's College, Banaras Hindu University, is a women's college in the Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, India which offers undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral courses in various subjects to women. It was founded in 1929 by Mahamana Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya.
Assi Ghat is the southernmost ghat in Varanasi, India. To most visitors to Varanasi, it is known for being a place where long-term foreign students, researchers, and tourists live.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ramnagar Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ramnagar Fort is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ramnagar Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, Varanasi (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Varanasi Junction (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ramnagar Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ramnagar Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ramnagar Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Ramnagar Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ramnagar Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ramnagar, Varanasi, Kachhua Sanctuary, Bhagawanpur, Varanasi, Durga Mandir, Ramnagar, Assi River.
